## Discussion

The [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplication/family] property determines how much space is available to a complication and which templates you can use to display your complication data. In your complication data source, you typically use the property’s value in a `switch` statement when determining the complication template your data source creates.

In watchOS 7 and later, ClockKit represents complications using its [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplication/family] and [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplication/identifier]. Each pair represents a unique complication that the user can select.

In watchOS 6 and earlier, ClockKit represents a complication by its [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplication/family] only. Each family can only have one complication. # family

**Instance Property**

The family to which the complication belongs.

## Declaration

```swift
var family: CLKComplicationFamily { get }
```

## Discussion

The [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplication/family] property determines how much space is available to a complication and which templates you can use to display your complication data. In your complication data source, you typically use the property’s value in a `switch` statement when determining the complication template your data source creates.

In watchOS 7 and later, ClockKit represents complications using its [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplication/family] and [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplication/identifier]. Each pair represents a unique complication that the user can select.

In watchOS 6 and earlier, ClockKit represents a complication by its [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/CLKComplication/family] only. Each family can only have one complication. For more information, see [doc://com.apple.clockkit/documentation/ClockKit/declaring-complications-for-your-app].

## Accessing Data About the Complication

- **identifier**: An identifier that specifies a complication if your app supports multiple complications per family.
- **userActivity**: An object that represents the state of the app at a moment in time.
- **userInfo**: A dictionary of additional data associated with the complication.
